mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate accelerates discount rate compression on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between month-over-month changes in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window, or conditional mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals deteriorating household income and consumption expectations, triggering risk-off repositioning in equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal fails to produce negative mean returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous elevation of consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate produces a joint drag on corporate real earnings growth and valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% do not produce negative average 60-day returns, or the interaction term carries a positive coefficient (p < 0.10) in a predictive regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, slowing industrial production growth predicts inventory liquidation and negative operating leverage, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods identified by UNRATE below 5% and flattening PAYEMS, INDPRO slowdowns fail to predict negative 20-day returns (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts that crosses below 1500 thousand units signals contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1500, or the conditional mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment alongside a restrictive federal funds rate reflects an overheating economy that compels additional monetary tightening, jointly depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of month-over-month PAYEMS increase and FEDFUNDS above 5% fails to generate negative average 60-day returns, or the interaction term in a linear model carries a positive coefficient (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding corporate operating leverage and earnings surprises, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth does not exhibit a positive Spearman correlation with 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of high-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A retail sales level below 380000 million dollars indicates weak consumer spending that compresses top-line revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with RSAFS below 380000 fail to produce negative average 60-day returns, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 50% over a 5-year walk-forward test.
